whyWear.headline: Performed in your name,
whyWear.intro: VIP pujas at Jyotirlinga temples are conducted by experienced pandits with sankalp in your name and gotra. You may also join the live stream for darshan from home.

benefits.title: Vedic sankalp
benefits.description: Puja performed exclusively in your name and family gotra.

benefits.title: Expert pandits
benefits.description: Schedules coordinated by priests with deep shrine experience.

benefits.title: Peace of mind
benefits.description: Ideal when travel to the Jyotirlinga is not possible.

wearingMantra.practice: Chant with devotion while the pandit performs sankalp on your behalf at the sacred shrine. Many families join the live stream from home, light a diya, and repeat the mantra eleven times as the ritual unfolds. This connects your name and gotra to the Jyotirlinga even when you cannot travel in person.

careRitual.headline: How to prepare for your puja.
careRitual.intro: Share correct name, gotra, and wish when booking. Join the live stream with a quiet space at home.

steps.title: Book
steps.description: Provide accurate name, gotra, and sankalp details when placing the order.

steps.title: Connect
steps.description: Answer the pandit’s call to confirm date, time, and special requests.

steps.title: Join
steps.description: Watch the live puja if a link is shared — light a diya at home in participation.

steps.title: Follow up
steps.description: Complete any prasad or charity suggestions given after the ritual.

faqs.question: How soon will the pandit contact me?
faqs.answer: After booking, our team or the assigned pandit usually contacts you within 24–48 hours to confirm your name, gotra, sankalp wish, and preferred date. During peak festival seasons, scheduling may take slightly longer — we will keep you informed by phone or WhatsApp. Please ensure your contact number is correct at checkout.

faqs.question: Can I watch the puja live?
faqs.answer: Yes — when a live link is available for your booked puja at the Jyotirlinga, it is shared before the ritual begins. You and your family can join from home, light a diya, and participate mentally while the pandit performs sankalp in your name. Recordings may be shared afterward depending on temple policy.

faqs.question: What details do I need to provide?
faqs.answer: Please provide your full name, gotra, and the intention or wish for the sankalp when booking. Some pujas also require birth details (date, time, place) for personalised sankalp — the pandit will confirm what is needed on the first call. Accurate details ensure the ritual is performed correctly in Vedic tradition.

faqs.question: Is prasad included?
faqs.answer: Prasad arrangements depend on the specific puja and temple. The pandit will explain whether prasad can be sent to your address, offered at the shrine on your behalf, or collected locally by a representative. Additional charges may apply for prasad courier — details are shared after booking confirmation.

whyWear.headline: Worn for centuries,
whyWear.intro: Rudraksha has been described in Vedic literature for over three thousand years. These are the four most cited benefits, drawn from the Padma Purana and Shiva Purana.

benefits.title: Peace of mind
benefits.description: Calms the nervous system; supports meditation and steadier sleep.

benefits.title: Clarity & focus
benefits.description: Sharpens concentration during study, work, and decision-making.

benefits.title: Spiritual protection
benefits.description: Long held to ward against negative energy and the evil eye.

benefits.title: Energy alignment
benefits.description: Balances the chakras; harmonises body and breath.

wearingMantra.practice: Salutations to the auspicious one. Recite eleven times each morning while holding the bracelet over the heart — this is the traditional energising practice for a freshly worn rudraksha.

careRitual.headline: How to wear your rudraksha.
careRitual.intro: A short four-step practice. Best done in the morning, after a bath, on a Monday — but begin whenever you can.

steps.title: Cleanse
steps.description: Soak overnight in raw cow milk, then rinse with Ganga jal or clean water. Pat dry with a soft cloth.

steps.title: Energise
steps.description: Hold in your right palm; recite Om Namaḥ Śivāya eleven times. Best done on a Monday morning after bath.

steps.title: Wear
steps.description: Tie on the right wrist with the knot facing inward. Many devotees keep it on continuously; remove during baths.

steps.title: Care
steps.description: Apply a drop of sandalwood oil weekly. Avoid soap, perfume, and chlorinated water to preserve the bead.

reviewsList.title: A daily anchor.
reviewsList.text: I have worn rudraksha bracelets before but this one feels different — the beads are clearly genuine and the leather has a beautiful rustic quality. It has become part of my morning routine.
reviewsList.author: Vikram Singh, Pune

reviewsList.title: Surprised by the quality.
reviewsList.text: The authenticity certificate and careful packaging gave me confidence. The Om centrepiece is beautifully detailed. My wife noticed the difference immediately.

reviewsList.text: Exactly what I wanted — not ostentatious, just quietly sacred. The rustic finish suits daily wear. I use it during morning meditation.

faqs.question: Is this rudraksha authentic?
faqs.answer: Every bead is X-ray and water-tested by an independent gemological lab in Mumbai, and ships with a numbered authenticity certificate where applicable. The bead must sink when placed in still water — a traditional test we still perform on every batch before dispatch. Astronext Divine Store sources rudraksha with care from trusted suppliers connected to Puri Dham.

faqs.question: Which wrist should I wear it on?
faqs.answer: Traditionally worn on the right wrist for men, as the right side is associated with active energy and Shiva worship in many lineages. Women may wear on the left wrist if preferred for comfort or tradition in your family. If unsure, consult your guru or follow the guidance included with your order.

faqs.question: Will it fade or lose its colour?
faqs.answer: Rudraksha and leather last longest when kept away from water, soap, lotion, and perfume. Apply a drop of sandalwood oil weekly on the beads, put the bracelet on last when dressing, and store in a dry zip pouch when not worn. Avoid chlorinated water and harsh chemicals to preserve both the bead and any plating.

faqs.question: Do you ship internationally?
faqs.answer: We currently ship across India from Puri with secure packaging. Free shipping applies on eligible orders above Rs. 499. For international delivery enquiries, please contact Astronext support — we are happy to advise on availability and customs for sacred items.

products.name: Shree Mahamrutyunjaya Yantra 6x6
products.descLong:
Shree Mahamrutyunjay Yantra

What is:   The Shree Mahamrutyunjay Yantra is dedicated to Lord Shiva, specifically to the aspect of Mahamrityunjaya, the conqueror of death. It is used for protection from life-threatening diseases, accidents, and premature death.

Why to use:   This yantra is used for health, long life, and protection from fatal accidents or illnesses.

How to use:   Place the yantra in your home or puja room and recite the Mahamrityunjaya mantra daily. It can also be kept near the bed of ailing individuals.

Benefits:
Protection from untimely death.
Ensures good health and longevity.
Alleviates fears of death and chronic illnesses.
Promotes mental peace and spiritual growth.
Enhances healing energy for the sick.

Other important points:   Worship of this yantra is most potent on Mondays and during the Shravan month, as these are considered auspiciou for Lord Shiva.

faqs.question: What size is the yantra?
faqs.answer: The dimensions are listed on each product page — most home yantras are laminated and sized for standard puja shelves or frames. The print is sharp enough for daily darshan at a comfortable viewing distance. If you need exact measurements for framing, contact support before ordering.

faqs.question: Which direction should it face?
faqs.answer: East is the traditional direction for yantra worship, aligning with the rising sun and auspicious beginnings. Place the yantra on a clean cloth at a respectful height, ideally where you sit for daily puja. Avoid placing it on the floor or in areas used for footwear or clutter.

faqs.question: Can I frame it?
faqs.answer: Yes — many devotees frame laminated yantras behind glass for long-term display on the altar. Use a frame with a small air gap in humid climates to prevent moisture trapping against the lamination. Do not expose framed yantras to direct sunlight for prolonged periods to avoid fading.

products.tagline: Hand-crafted rudraksha, blessed for daily wear.

whyWear.headline: Worn for centuries,
whyWear.intro: Rudraksha has been described in Vedic literature for over three thousand years. These are the four most cited benefits, drawn from the Padma Purana and Shiva Purana.

benefits.title: Peace of mind
benefits.description: Calms the nervous system; supports meditation and steadier sleep.

benefits.title: Clarity & focus
benefits.description: Sharpens concentration during study, work, and decision-making.

benefits.title: Spiritual protection
benefits.description: Long held to ward against negative energy and the evil eye.

benefits.title: Energy alignment
benefits.description: Balances the chakras; harmonises body and breath.

wearingMantra.practice: Salutations to the auspicious one. Recite eleven times each morning while holding the bracelet over the heart — this is the traditional energising practice for a freshly worn rudraksha.

careRitual.headline: How to wear your rudraksha.
careRitual.intro: A short four-step practice. Best done in the morning, after a bath, on a Monday — but begin whenever you can.

steps.title: Cleanse
steps.description: Soak overnight in raw cow milk, then rinse with Ganga jal or clean water. Pat dry with a soft cloth.

steps.title: Energise
steps.description: Hold in your right palm; recite Om Namaḥ Śivāya eleven times. Best done on a Monday morning after bath.

steps.title: Wear
steps.description: Tie on the right wrist with the knot facing inward. Many devotees keep it on continuously; remove during baths.

steps.title: Care
steps.description: Apply a drop of sandalwood oil weekly. Avoid soap, perfume, and chlorinated water to preserve the bead.

faqs.question: Is this rudraksha authentic?
faqs.answer: Every bead is X-ray and water-tested by an independent gemological lab in Mumbai, and ships with a numbered authenticity certificate where applicable. The bead must sink when placed in still water — a traditional test we still perform on every batch before dispatch. Astronext Divine Store sources rudraksha with care from trusted suppliers connected to Puri Dham.

faqs.question: Which wrist should I wear it on?
faqs.answer: Traditionally worn on the right wrist for men, as the right side is associated with active energy and Shiva worship in many lineages. Women may wear on the left wrist if preferred for comfort or tradition in your family. If unsure, consult your guru or follow the guidance included with your order.

faqs.question: Will it fade or lose its colour?
faqs.answer: Rudraksha and leather last longest when kept away from water, soap, lotion, and perfume. Apply a drop of sandalwood oil weekly on the beads, put the bracelet on last when dressing, and store in a dry zip pouch when not worn. Avoid chlorinated water and harsh chemicals to preserve both the bead and any plating.

faqs.question: Do you ship internationally?
faqs.answer: We currently ship across India from Puri with secure packaging. Free shipping applies on eligible orders above Rs. 499. For international delivery enquiries, please contact Astronext support — we are happy to advise on availability and customs for sacred items.

products.tagline: Hand-crafted rudraksha, blessed for daily wear.

whyWear.headline: Worn for centuries,
whyWear.intro: Rudraksha has been described in Vedic literature for over three thousand years. These are the four most cited benefits, drawn from the Padma Purana and Shiva Purana.

benefits.title: Peace of mind
benefits.description: Calms the nervous system; supports meditation and steadier sleep.

benefits.title: Clarity & focus
benefits.description: Sharpens concentration during study, work, and decision-making.

benefits.title: Spiritual protection
benefits.description: Long held to ward against negative energy and the evil eye.

benefits.title: Energy alignment
benefits.description: Balances the chakras; harmonises body and breath.

wearingMantra.practice: Salutations to the auspicious one. Recite eleven times each morning while holding the bracelet over the heart — this is the traditional energising practice for a freshly worn rudraksha.

careRitual.headline: How to wear your rudraksha.
careRitual.intro: A short four-step practice. Best done in the morning, after a bath, on a Monday — but begin whenever you can.

steps.title: Cleanse
steps.description: Soak overnight in raw cow milk, then rinse with Ganga jal or clean water. Pat dry with a soft cloth.

steps.title: Energise
steps.description: Hold in your right palm; recite Om Namaḥ Śivāya eleven times. Best done on a Monday morning after bath.

steps.title: Wear
steps.description: Tie on the right wrist with the knot facing inward. Many devotees keep it on continuously; remove during baths.

steps.title: Care
steps.description: Apply a drop of sandalwood oil weekly. Avoid soap, perfume, and chlorinated water to preserve the bead.

faqs.question: Is this rudraksha authentic?
faqs.answer: Every bead is X-ray and water-tested by an independent gemological lab in Mumbai, and ships with a numbered authenticity certificate where applicable. The bead must sink when placed in still water — a traditional test we still perform on every batch before dispatch. Astronext Divine Store sources rudraksha with care from trusted suppliers connected to Puri Dham.

faqs.question: Which wrist should I wear it on?
faqs.answer: Traditionally worn on the right wrist for men, as the right side is associated with active energy and Shiva worship in many lineages. Women may wear on the left wrist if preferred for comfort or tradition in your family. If unsure, consult your guru or follow the guidance included with your order.

faqs.question: Will it fade or lose its colour?
faqs.answer: Rudraksha and leather last longest when kept away from water, soap, lotion, and perfume. Apply a drop of sandalwood oil weekly on the beads, put the bracelet on last when dressing, and store in a dry zip pouch when not worn. Avoid chlorinated water and harsh chemicals to preserve both the bead and any plating.

faqs.question: Do you ship internationally?
faqs.answer: We currently ship across India from Puri with secure packaging. Free shipping applies on eligible orders above Rs. 499. For international delivery enquiries, please contact Astronext support — we are happy to advise on availability and customs for sacred items.
